original_question:
For all positive integers $n$, the $n$th hexagonal number $H_n$ is defined as $H_n = 2n^2 - n$. What is the greatest possible value of the greatest common divisor of $3H_n$ and $n+1$?

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: For all positive integers $n$, the $n$th hexagonal number $H_n$ is defined as $H_n = 3n^2 - 2n$. What is the greatest possible value of the greatest common divisor of $4H_n$ and $n+2$?

For all positive integers $n$, the $n$th hexagonal number $H_n$ is defined as $H_n = 4n^2 - 2n$. What is the greatest possible value of the greatest common divisor of $5H_n$ and $n+4$?
For all positive integers $n$, the $n$th hexagonal number $H_n$ is defined as $H_n = 6n^2 - 4n$. What is the greatest possible value of the greatest common divisor of $3H_n$ and $n+5$?
For all positive integers $n$, the $n$th hexagonal number $H_n$ is defined as $H_n = 7n^2 - 5n$. What is the greatest possible value of the greatest common divisor of $6H_n$ and $n+6$?
